level_4_sections.summary: # This function creates a file in the specified bucket. $1 - The name of the bucket to copy the file to. $2 - The path and file name of the local file to copy to the bucket. $3 - The key (name) to call the copy of the file in the bucket. function copy_file_to_bucket() { local response bucket_name source_file destination_file_name bucket_name=$1 source_file=$2 destination_file_name=$3 response=$(aws s3api put-object --key "$destination_file_name") errecho "ERROR: AWS reports put-object operation failed.\n$response" For API details, see PutObject in AWS CLI Command Reference. bool AwsDoc::S3::putObject(const Aws::String &bucketName, const Aws::String &fileName, const Aws::S3::S3ClientConfiguration &clientConfig) { Aws::S3::Model::PutObjectRequest request; //We are using the name of the file as the key for the object in the bucket. //However, this is just a string and can be set according to your retrieval needs. request.SetKey(fileName); std::shared_ptr<Aws::IOStream> inputData = Aws::MakeShared<Aws::FStream>("SampleAllocationTag", fileName.c_str(), std::ios_base::in | std::ios_base::binary); if (! *inputData) { std::cerr << "Error unable to read file " << fileName << std::endl; request.SetBody(inputData); Aws::S3::Model::PutObjectOutcome outcome = s3Client.PutObject(request); std::cerr << "Error: putObject: " << std::cout << "Added object '" << fileName << "' to bucket '" << bucketName << "'. "; Example 1: Upload an object to Amazon S3 The following put-object command example uploads an object to Amazon S3. // UploadObject uses the S3 upload manager to upload an object to a bucket. @param bucketName The name of the Amazon S3 bucket. public static void putS3ObjectTags(S3Client s3, String bucketName, String objectKey, String objectPath) { Tag tag1 = Tag.builder().key("Tag 1").value("This is tag 1") Tag tag2 = Tag.builder().key("Tag 2").value("This is tag 2") tags.add(tag1); tags.add(tag2); Tagging allTags = Tagging.builder().tagSet(tags) PutObjectRequest putOb = PutObjectRequest.builder().tagging(allTags) s3.putObject(putOb, RequestBody.fromBytes(getObjectFile(objectPath))); System.err.println(e.getMessage()); Updates the tags associated with an object in an Amazon S3 bucket. objectPath - The path where the file is located (for example, C:/ AWS/book2.pdf).\s if (args.length != 3) { System.out.println(USAGE); String objectKey = args[1]; String objectPath = args[2]; System.out.println("Putting object " + objectKey + " into bucket " + System.out.println(" in bucket: " + bucketName); putS3Object(s3, bucketName, objectKey, objectPath); Uploads an object to an Amazon S3 bucket with metadata. import software.amazon.awssdk.services.s3.model.PutObjectRetentionRequest; import software.amazon.awssdk.services.s3.model.ObjectLockRetention; import java.time.Instant; import java.time.LocalDate; import java.time.LocalDateTime; import java.time.ZoneOffset; public class PutObjectRetention { key - The name of the object (for example, book.pdf).\s bucketName - The Amazon S3 bucket name that contains the object (for example, bucket1).\s String key = args[0]; String bucketName = args[1]; setRentionPeriod(s3, key, bucketName); Sets the retention period for an object in an Amazon S3 bucket. period public static void setRentionPeriod(S3Client s3, String key, String bucket) { LocalDate localDate = LocalDate.parse("2020-07-17"); LocalDateTime localDateTime = localDate.atStartOfDay(); Instant instant = localDateTime.toInstant(ZoneOffset.UTC); ObjectLockRetention lockRetention = ObjectLockRetention.builder().mode("COMPLIANCE").retainUntilDate(instant) PutObjectRetentionRequest retentionRequest = PutObjectRetentionRequest.builder().bypassGovernanceRetention(true).retention(lockRetention) // To set Retention on an object, the Amazon S3 bucket must support // locking, otherwise an exception is thrown. "); PutObjectRetentionCommand, Place a 24-hour retention period on an object in an Amazon S3 bucket. public class RestoreArchivedObject string objectKey = "archived-object.txt"; RegionEndpoint bucketRegion = RegionEndpoint.USWest2; IAmazonS3 client = new AmazonS3Client(bucketRegion); RestoreObjectAsync(client, bucketName, objectKey).Wait(); /// This method restores an archived object from an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon S3 buckets are named in a. global namespace; use this method to determine if a specified bucket name already exists, and therefore can't. public class GenPresignedUrl const string bucketName = "amzn-s3-demo-bucket"; const string objectKey = "sample.txt"; // Specify how long the presigned URL lasts, in hours const double timeoutDuration = 12; // Specify the AWS Region of your Amazon S3 bucket. double timeoutDuration = 12; // Specify the AWS Region of your Amazon S3 bucket. /// GetPreSignedURL.</param> /// <param name="bucketName">The name of the Amazon S3 bucket to which /// presigned URL will point.</param> /// <param name="objectKey">The name of the file that will be uploaded.</ /// <param name="duration">How long (in hours) the presigned URL will /// be valid.</param> public static string GeneratePreSignedURL( string objectKey, double duration) var request = new GetPreSignedUrlRequest Verb = HttpVerb.PUT, Expires = DateTime.UtcNow.AddHours(duration), string url = client.GetPreSignedURL(request); return url Create and use presigned POST URLs for direct browser uploads.
